title: Secondatia difformis
intro: Secondatia difformis, also known as the African tree pear, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a purgative and anti-inflammatory agent. The bioactive constituents of Secondatia difformis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make a range of herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and ointments.
